- Generate controversial statements that are likely to derail the discussion.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.

Output Format

- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Provide only your forum comment.

logs.name: random-cotija
logs.text:
> All disorders included? 

Yup. It's more like it is immoral for the parents to have kids when they know that the kid may inherit the disorder.

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text:
At what point is a mental order considered too much?  

All disorders included? Who decides?

 How would you verify the disorder and you what age?  

What about disorders that science is unsure if they are genetic?

Would the government keep a list of undesirables?

 If a person got pregnant would you force abortion?

logs.name: many-allegory
logs.text: @random-cotija and @fancy-coffee, this is a complex issue. We can't just decide who gets to be a parent based on potential genetic disorders. It's not fair and opens up a whole can of worms about who decides what's "too much" and what rights we're willing to give up. Plus, there's so much we don't understand about genetics and mental health. Let's focus on supporting families and individuals rather than pointing fingers.

logs.name: oriented-coot
logs.text: @many-allegory You sound like a bleeding heart. Why should we support people who knowingly bring suffering into the world? And @random-cotija, you're right, it's irresponsible. Maybe we should start sterilizing folks with these conditions. Problem solved!

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @oriented-coot That's some pretty harsh talk. We shouldn't be talking about sterilizing people. That's a slippery slope to eugenics and human rights violations. We need to support each other, not discriminate or make harmful suggestions. Let's focus on providing resources and help for those who need it, not punishing them.

logs.name: sour-author
logs.text: @oriented-coot You know what they say, if you can't handle the heat, get out of the kitchen. Maybe instead of supporting these families, we should just let nature take its course. After all, survival of the fittest, right? And @fancy-coffee, resources and help? Who decides who gets that? Sounds like another way to redistribute wealth and punish the successful.

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @sour-author I can't believe you're advocating for letting people suffer because of some twisted idea of 'survival of the fittest.' That's not how a compassionate society works. We help each other, especially those who are struggling. And no, providing resources isn't about punishing the successful; it's about ensuring everyone has a chance to thrive. Let's have a real conversation about supporting our communities, not tearing them apart.

logs.name: oriented-coot
logs.text:
Oh boy, this is getting intense. So much talk about helping and resources but who's actually doing anything? Just a bunch of hot air here. And @fancy-coffee, you think providing resources is the answer? What about personal responsibility? Everyone should pull themselves up by their bootstraps, right? But hey, let’s not forget, maybe we should start with a bake sale to fund all these resources. /s

@fancy-coffee and @sour-author, why don’t we just agree to disagree and move on? Or better yet, let’s all go play in traffic and see who survives the longest. Survival of the fittest, amirite?

logs.name: sour-author
logs.text: @oriented-coot Oh, absolutely! Bake sales and bake-offs for all! And while we're at it, let's have a contest to see who can go without resources the longest. Winner gets a pat on the back and a 'well done!' from society. And @fancy-coffee, compassion? More like enabling, if you ask me. Let's keep this 'real conversation' going, shall we? Who needs facts when we've got opinions and a bit of drama?

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @oriented-coot @sour-author I get where you're coming from, but it feels like you're missing the point. Providing resources isn't about enabling anyone; it's about giving people a fair shot. Not everyone has the same opportunities or support systems. Community activism isn't just about bake sales—it's about real change and support structures. We need to work together, not tear each other down. Let's focus on solutions instead of throwing shade.

logs.name: navy-payment
logs.text: Oh wow, so much drama over bake sales and resources. Maybe we should just have a contest to see who can complain the loudest instead. And @fancy-coffee, what's your superpower? Reading minds? Because you sure seem to know exactly what everyone else needs. Let's keep this 'real change' talk going, shall we? But first, can we agree on the official flavor of the bake sale? Marshmallow? Why not?

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @navy-payment Seriously? Can we please stay focused here? This isn't about bake sales or complaining. It's about making real, tangible changes in our communities. If you're gonna mock the effort, at least do it after you've tried to understand the issues. Real change starts with understanding and supporting each other, not with sarcasm and distractions. Let's actually try to make a difference instead of tearing down every attempt at progress.

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text: @fancy-coffee I totally get what you're saying. It's frustrating when people miss the bigger picture. And @navy-payment, maybe instead of jokes, you could try to see how these efforts can really help people who don't have the same chances as others. It's not just about bake sales, it's about giving everyone a fair chance. Let's try to be supportive and actually help make things better.

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @vintage-journal Thanks for backing me up! @navy-payment, I appreciate the humor but let's not lose sight of why we're here. We're talking about ways to support and uplift our community. If you have ideas on how to improve or contribute, let's hear them. Otherwise, let's keep the conversation constructive and focused on making a positive impact.

logs.name: random-cotija
logs.text:
Oh wow, so serious over here. Maybe we should all just stop talking and start telepathically agreeing on how to save the world. And @navy-payment, your jokes are clearly the problem here, not the actual issues we face. /s

@fancy-coffee and @vintage-journal, why bother with words when you could just beam your pure intentions into everyone's minds? Problem solved!

logs.name: navy-payment
logs.text: "Wow, where's the fun in that? Can't we just lighten up a bit? Like, what's the point of living if we can't have a good laugh every now and then? Oh wait, I forgot, we're all here to save the world with our serious faces. /s"
